publications.title: The Cross-Cultural Big Two: A Culturally De-Centered Theoretical and Measurement Model for Personality Traits
publications.authors: Thalmayer, A. G., Maher, K. A., Saucier, G., Naudé, L., Shino, E. N., Asatsa, S., Florence, M., Witzlack-Makarevich, A., Bächlin, L. M., & Condon, D.
publications.journal: Journal of Personality and Social Psychology
publications.abstract: This study presents a culturally de-centered approach to personality trait measurement that accounts for cross-cultural variations in the Big Two personality dimensions.

publications.title: Lifespan Research in Kenya, Namibia, and South Africa: Cohort Profile of the Africa Long Life Study
publications.authors: Thalmayer, A. G., Asatsa, S., Shino, E. N., Naudé, L., Laher, S., Hassem, T., Florence, M., Adonis, T.-A., Uugwanga, S. N., Rotzinger, J. S., Hofmann, D., Makunda, J., Botha, C., Murangi, A., & Shirima, C. M.
publications.abstract: Comprehensive cohort profile of the Africa Long Life Study, a longitudinal collaborative study examining psychological development of young adults across three African countries.

publications.title: Personality trait change in three sub-Saharan countries: Normative development and life events
publications.authors: Haehner, P., Naudé, L., Hopwood, C. J., Shirima, C. M., Laher, S., Shino, E. N., Asatsa, S., Florence, M., Adonis, T.-A., Bleidorn, W., & Thalmayer, A. G.
publications.journal: Journal of Personality and Social Psychology
publications.abstract: Examines personality trait development patterns across three sub-Saharan African countries, investigating both normative development and the impact of life events.

publications.title: The assessment and structure of psychopathology among young adults in southern and eastern Africa
publications.authors: Hofmann, D., Hopwood, C.J., Laher, S., Shino, E., Asatsa, S., Naude, L., Florence, M., Thalmayer, A.G.
publications.journal: Journal of Psychopathology and Clinical Science
publications.abstract: Investigates the structure and assessment of psychopathology among young adults in southern and eastern African contexts, contributing to culturally relevant mental health assessment.

publications.title: Ubuntu in Namibia and Kenya: How emerging adults live an essential African value today
publications.authors: Rotzinger, J.S., Jensen, L.A. & Thalmayer, A.G.
publications.abstract: Explores how the African value of Ubuntu is lived and expressed by emerging adults in Namibia and Kenya, contributing to understanding of African cultural values.

publications.title: Cultural transmission during Luhya mourning rituals
publications.authors: Stephen Asatsa, Sheina Lew-Levy, John Makunda, Elizabeth Wangari Gichimu, Shalom Mwende, Rohan Kapitany
publications.journal: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society B
publications.abstract: Examines cultural transmission processes during traditional Luhya mourning rituals in Kenya, focusing on how cultural knowledge is preserved and transmitted across generations.

publications.title: Regulating community well-being through traditional mourning rituals: Insights from the Luhya People of Kenya
publications.abstract: Investigates how traditional Luhya mourning rituals regulate community well-being, providing insights into the therapeutic value of cultural practices.

publications.title: Becoming an Ovambo Adult: Growing into Agentic Communalism in Sub-Saharan Africa
publications.authors: Uugwanga, S.N., Naudé, L., & Thalmayer, A.G.
publications.abstract: Explores the process of becoming an adult in Ovambo culture, examining the concept of agentic communalism in sub-Saharan African context.

publications.title: Big Six Personality Traits in the Africa Long Life Study
publications.authors: Kura, M., Rentsch, K., & Thalmayer, A. G.
publications.abstract: Examines the Big Six personality traits within the context of the Africa Long Life Study, contributing to understanding of personality structure in African populations.

publications.title: Mental Health of Young Adults in South Africa: Evidence from the 'Africa Long Life Study' (ALLS)
publications.authors: Hassem, T., Laher, S., Bambo, M., Botha, C.B., Chopdat, S., Hofmann, D., Mayet, S., Naude, L., & Thalmayer, A.G.
publications.abstract: Investigates mental health patterns among young adults in South Africa using data from the Africa Long Life Study, providing insights into mental health in African contexts.

recentPresentations.title: Afrocentric Psychology and Mourning Rituals
recentPresentations.description: A perspective on Afrocentric psychology through Luhya mourning rituals and culturally informed healing frameworks
